items.rationale: Believes crypto creates permissionless markets for compute/data that democratize AI and add provenance/transparency against deep fakes and black-box models.
items.age_note: First stated 2023-12-06, restated/expanded 2024-04-19 — current and consistent.

citations.quote: Decentralized blockchains are a counterbalancing force to centralized AI.
We can democratize AI innovation by using blockchain networks and crypto technology to decentralize access to key resources.

items.rationale: Sees Lasso/Jolt as a new SNARK paradigm and a significant step toward the 'endgame solution' for scaling, with open-source implementation.
items.age_note: Recurring excitement from 2023-08-10 through 2024-04-09; most recent is strong.

citations.quote: This is a significant contribution toward the endgame solution for blockchain scalability, and of course the implementation is fully open source
Lasso and Jolt represent a new paradigm in SNARK design.

items.rationale: Tracks non-financial adoption hitting all-time highs (mobile wallets, active addresses); reads UX improvements as pulling mainstream users in with 20x headroom.

citations.quote: The number of mobile wallet users in crypto is now at an all time high of 28 million
March was a breakout month with 27M active mobile wallet users

items.rationale: Notes low-transaction-fee chains capturing majority of DEX volume and leading active-address growth — evidence cheap blockspace drives real usage.

citations.quote: low-transaction fee blockchains like @base and @solana captured a majority of this activity
37M active addresses across all tracked blockchains, a 34% month-over-month growth. The top chain... @solana

items.rationale: Views blockchains as a new global payment standard that removes middlemen — near-free, instant, global settlement on a $647B remittance market.

citations.quote: Payments can be extremely cheap (<$0.01), instantly settled, and global by default - just like sending an email.

items.rationale: Excited by progress toward resilient, censorship-resistant social protocols where users own and port their data and developers can't be deplatformed.

citations.quote: A huge milestone crossed on the path toward more resilient, decentralized social networks for the world.
Decentralized social networks cannot unilaterally make decisions or change their rules for users or developers.

items.rationale: Sees blockchains coordinating idle physical resources (GPUs, storage, telecom) to unlock supply and democratize access at lower cost.
items.age_note: Single substantive mention (2024-04-19) within his areas-of-impact thread.

citations.quote: Using blockchain networks to coordinate a broader set of participants, we can unlock additional resources and democratize access to them.

items.rationale: Frames staking as securing a large on-chain economy, not just yield; praised Lido's decentralization commitment and the Staking Router.
items.age_note: Last addressed 2023-04-20 (Lido 2023-02-07); not restated in 2024 — persisted, aging.

citations.quote: Staking is not just about earning yield. On Ethereum, it's used to secure a $400 billion economy of ETH, ERC20 tokens, and NFTs.
The Staking Router is huge step forward. Lido's commitment to decentralization has been unwavering.

items.rationale: Excited about expanding legal and design space for token/reputation-based governance enabling trustless coordination at scale.

citations.quote: Huge news for expanding the ways in which DAOs can legally operate.

items.rationale: Worries the US is losing its web3 lead; wants regulatory clarity to keep development onshore, comparing dependence on foreign tech to semiconductors.
items.age_note: Sustained theme 2023-11-17 → 2024-05-14; most recent is hopeful about getting clarity.

citations.quote: Keeping crypto onshore is a matter of national security. Will the U.S. learn from past experiences like semiconductor manufacturing?
The crypto industry has a real chance to get the regulatory clarity we deserve.

items.rationale: Sees passive ownership vs. actual usage as the bigger problem than expanding investor count; activation, not acquisition, is the growth bottleneck.

citations.quote: Let's close the gap between crypto owners and active on-chain users. This is more important... than expanding the total number of crypto investors.

items.rationale: Concerned a handful of tech giants control AI compute/data and that web traffic/market cap is concentrating, plus a coming flood of AI deep fakes.

citations.quote: 3 companies now generate a third of all global web traffic. 5 companies represent 50% of the Nasdaq 100's market cap.
it also creates a world of abundant AI-generated content, including deep fakes.

items.rationale: Views the unsolved difficulty of enforcing NFT royalties as a real drag that has held the sector back, though he sees promising new design ideas.

citations.quote: Enforcing royalties on NFT sales has always been tricky, and it's held the industry back.

items.rationale: Leading chain by monthly active addresses and a top capturer of record DEX volume; Phantom among the fastest-growing wallets — strongest usage signal in his data.

citations.quote: The top chain by monthly active addresses: @solana.
newer mobile wallets like @phantom have been growing the fastest in recent months.

items.rationale: Treats ETH as securing a large multi-asset on-chain economy via staking; constructive on its post-Shanghai staking dynamics.
items.age_note: Core positive framing dates to 2023-04-20; not refreshed with a 2024 ETH-specific view.

citations.quote: On Ethereum, it's used to secure a $400 billion economy of ETH, ERC20 tokens, and NFTs.

risk_regime.rationale: Consistently constructive, but his bullishness rests on rising non-financial adoption metrics (State of Crypto Index at all-time highs, active addresses, mobile wallets) rather than price. He explicitly de-emphasizes price speculation as a byproduct separate from core value, so his risk-on posture is structural/adoption-driven, not a tactical bet on token markets. Main hedge is US regulatory uncertainty.

notes: Daren is a16z crypto's data/metrics voice (owner of the State of Crypto Index), so his views skew toward measurable adoption and infrastructure (ZK/SNARKs, wallets, low-fee chains) over token price calls — hence an empty tokens_concerned and no individual token bear cases. He repeatedly separates speculation from crypto's 'core value proposition' (2024-03-21), which colors the structural framing of nearly every entry. Token-level views are sparse: ETH/SOL are inferred from his adoption commentary, not direct investment theses. His most recent statement (2024-06-26) on NFT royalties is the basis for treating NFTs as a concern (an unsolved design problem) rather than an excitement. Regulation/national-security is his single most-repeated worry across 18 months and trends slightly more optimistic in the most recent (2024-05-14) instance. No material reversals detected; older staking/Ethereum stances are persisted and flagged by age.
